diagrams.title: Basic Computer System Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows the fundamental structure of a computer with CPU, memory, and I/O connected via system bus
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how components communicate and the fetch-decode-execute cycle flow

diagrams.title: Memory Hierarchy Pyramid
diagrams.description: Illustrates the layered memory structure from fastest to slowest
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the speed-cost-capacity tradeoff in memory design and understanding locality principles

diagrams.title: Instruction Cycle State Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the three states of instruction processing with sub-operations
elements: Execute Cycle (ALU operation, Result to ACC/Memory)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how CPU processes each instruction step-by-step and the role of registers in each phase

diagrams.title: CPU-Memory-I/O Interaction
diagrams.description: Shows the complete data flow when executing a memory read/write operation
elements: CPU sends address via address bus
Data bus transfers data between memory and MDR
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how the three main components interact during basic operations and the role of buses
